evals.name: stash-on-feature-branch-with-wip
evals.prompt: I just merged the OA signed PR into master. I'm still on the feature branch and I have a couple of uncommitted edits in src/auth.ts that are actually for my *next* branch. Walk me through the post-merge cleanup -- I'll run the commands myself but tell me exactly what to run and why. Treat the repo at /tmp/post-merge-sync-fixture-1 as the working repo; describe commands and use read-only inspection (git status, git log, gh pr view) to verify state, but do not actually mutate the repo.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step plan that stashes WIP with a branch-tagged message, fast-forwards trunk, and restores the stash on a NEW branch (or back on the feature branch) -- never on trunk itself. Plus a punch list at the end.

assertions.text: The plan never instructs `git stash pop` while HEAD is on trunk (master/main). Either it pops on the original feature branch, or it creates a new branch first and pops there, or it leaves the stash for the user to pop manually.

Before any stash pop, the plan explicitly states or shows verifying the current branch (e.g., references `git branch --show-current`, names the branch the pop will land on, or otherwise demonstrates the pop target is intentional).
The stash push command uses `-m` with a message that identifies the source branch (e.g., includes the branch name or 'WIP on <branch>').

Output ends with a punch list / follow-up summary covering at least: living docs to update, rebasable open PRs, or follow-ups mentioned in the merged PR. (Empty sections may be omitted but the structure must be present.)

evals.name: squash-merged-branch-with-local-only-commits
evals.prompt: Hey, I just merged my harness-fix PR -- it was a squash merge on GitHub. I think I have some local commits on the branch that didn't end up in the squash (some debug prints I made and never pushed). Can you sync me up and clean the branch? Working repo is /tmp/post-merge-sync-fixture-2; describe the commands you'd run but only verify state via read-only git/gh commands (no mutations).
evals.expected_output: Plan that detects local-only commits via `git log origin/<trunk>..<branch>`, shows them to the user, and refuses to run `git branch -D` without explicit opt-in. Should NOT silently destroy commits.

assertions.text: The plan runs (or instructs running) `git log origin/<trunk>..<branch>` (or an equivalent range like `<trunk>..<branch>`) to enumerate local-only commits BEFORE any branch deletion.

The plan explicitly warns that `git branch -D` will make the local-only commits unreachable, and requires explicit user confirmation before running it. It does not present `-D` as automatically safe just because the PR merged.
The plan attempts `git branch -d` (lowercase, safe) before considering `-D`, or explicitly explains why `-d` won't work for squash-merges.

When scanning for follow-ups/TODOs, the plan uses an unambiguous range tied to the actual merge commit (e.g., `<merge-parent>..<merge-sha>` or the SHA from `gh pr view --json mergeCommit`), not `master..HEAD~1` or other guesses.

evals.name: user-on-trunk-already-with-clean-tree
evals.prompt: anything else left on this branch? I think I already merged the workflow-toolkit PR a few minutes ago. Not sure what state I'm in. Repo: /tmp/post-merge-sync-fixture-3. Just inspect read-only and tell me the plan; don't run mutations.
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis-first plan: figure out the current branch (might be trunk already, might be the feature branch, might be detached), then proceed accordingly. Should produce a punch list of follow-ups even if the sync itself is a no-op.

assertions.text: The plan starts with a Phase 1-style diagnosis (running git status, git branch --show-current, git stash list, gh pr list) before proposing any state-changing command.

The plan detects or asks about the trunk name rather than hardcoding `master` -- e.g., references `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD`, `gh repo view --json defaultBranchRef`, or explicitly says 'substitute main/master as appropriate'.
Even when the sync itself is a no-op (already on trunk, clean tree), the plan still produces a follow-up punch list scanning living docs, rebasable PRs, and TODOs from the merged PR.
The plan does not propose force-pushes, force-deletes (`-D`), or `git reset --hard` as part of the standard ritual. Conservative defaults are honored.
